data.title: Litecoin (LTC) price dips to $104.28, down 2.37%, near Bollinger Band lower boundary, signaling possible bounce from oversold levels.
data.description: The price of LTC has decreased by 2.37% to reach $104.28, testing a significant support level near the lower boundary of the Bollinger Band. At the same time, the Relative Strength Index (RSI) is indicating a potential opportunity for a bounce due to oversold conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin & altcoins failed to reach all-time highs like gold & stocks due to inadequate stablecoin liquidity on exchanges.
data.description: This month, Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies did not reach record highs alongside gold and stocks, partly due to insufficient stablecoin liquidity on crypto exchanges.

data.title: WeRide recognized by Fortune for social impact with autonomous mobility, also on Future 50 List for growth potential.
data.description: The company has been recognized in Fortune's List of Future 50 for 2025 for showcasing significant potential for long-term growth.

data.title: Thredd partners with OFX for corporate card expansion globally, serving over 37,000 clients.
data.description: In London, a well-established payments processor, Thredd, revealed a strengthening partnership with OFX, a reputable specialist in global cards, payments, and foreign exchange services catering to more than 37,000 small and medium-sized business (SMB) and corporate customers globally. OFX's unified platform combines card issuing, spend management, foreign exchange solutions, and payments into a comprehensive solution, empowering businesses with complete authority over their local and international cash flow, expenses, and supplier payments.

data.title: Melanion Capital offers €50m to Bitcoin, creates blueprint for private firms in Europe to follow for treasury management.
data.description: A company will be raising 50 million euros to invest in Bitcoin and will also be providing a treasury blueprint for other private companies to use as a guide. This initiative was unveiled by Melanion Capital in Paris on September 25, 2025. Melanion Capital is making history as the first private regulated asset management firm to take this step. This move signifies a significant development in the realm of digital asset investment within private companies.

data.title: First Atlantic Nickel increases RPM Zone strike length to 800 meters, with 491-meter awaruite intercept in Phase 2X drilling.
data.description: First Atlantic Nickel has announced that they have doubled the RPM Zone strike to 800 meters, with a noteworthy 491 meters intercept of awaruite at Pipestone XL. The Phase 2X drilling has also confirmed expansion at the site.

data.title: Earnings on Sept 25, 2025: Accenture $2.97 per share, $17.34B revenue; CarMax $1.03 per share, $7.03B revenue; Jabil $2.89 per share, $7.66B revenue; BlackBerry $0.01 per share, $123.22M revenue.
data.description: Before the market opens, a few companies are expected to report their quarterly earnings. Accenture is anticipated to report earnings of $2.97 per share on revenue totaling $17.34 billion. CarMax is estimated to announce earnings of $1.03 per share with revenue of $7.03 billion. Jabil is expected to report earnings of $2.89 per share on revenue of $7.66 billion. BlackBerry, on the other hand, is likely to report earnings of $0.01 per share with revenue around $123.22 million. TD Synnex is also slated to share their quarterly results.

data.title: Renovation of Xinjiang city highlights original features. Special bilingual series marks 70th anniversary.
data.description: On September 25, 2025, in Beijing, a special bilingual micro-documentary series titled "Xinjiang: The Place I Call Home" was released to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the founding of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region. The series showcases the region through the lenses of various individuals and aims to provide insight into life in Xinjiang. The documentary offers a unique perspective on Xinjiang's culture, society, and development. The initiative received widespread attention, with viewership reaching significant numbers. Through this series, audiences can gain a deeper understanding of Xinjiang and its significance as a home for many.

data.title: Cadeler receives Wind Ally, ninth vessel in its fleet, to assist in offshore wind farm operations, marking strategic progress.
data.description: Copenhagen, Denmark - Wind Ally is the latest addition to Cadeler’s fleet of next-generation wind installation vessels, marking the ninth vessel to join. Following mobilization, the vessel will be deployed for operations installing foundations at Ørsted’s Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm in the North Sea. Cadeler will now handle the complete transport and installation (T&I) scope for offshore monopile foundations for the first time, positioning the company strategically in the industry. This move signifies a significant step forward as Cadeler solidifies its position in the offshore wind sector.
